S1.title: NLS focusing bright soliton — Madelung-Psi split-step validation
S1.ic: Psi(x,0) = sqrt(2) * sech(sqrt(2)*(x+5)) * exp(i*0.25*x). (Bright soliton, amplitude sqrt(2), at x=-5, moving at speed v/2 = 0.25.)
S1.ground_truth: Exact sech bright soliton, mass(N) = 2*sqrt(2) exactly conserved, peak amplitude = 2 exactly conserved, peak position translates at speed 0.25, no shape change.
S1.stress_question: What is the minimum dt for split-step Fourier accuracy? At what Nx does the method fail? Does it conserve mass to machine precision?
S1.extra_method_to_try: After split-step Fourier works, try direct (N, phi) evolution via FFT (no Psi reconstruction) to compare; report relative accuracy.

S2.title: NLS focusing — direct (N, phi) vs Madelung-Psi methods
S2.ic: Same IC as S1 (bright sech soliton).
S2.stress_question: Does direct (N, phi) evolution succeed? At what point does the quantum pressure (sqrt(N))_xx / (2 sqrt(N)) singularity affect accuracy or stability? What is the failure mode if you do NOT use Madelung-Psi transform?
S2.parameters_to_sweep: Try at least two methods: (a) direct spectral FFT on (N, phi) with quantum pressure computed directly, (b) Madelung-Psi split-step. Report which works, what fails, and ANY parameter regimes where they diverge.

S3.title: NLS focusing Gaussian — modulational instability and soliton emission threshold
S3.ic: Psi(x,0) = A * exp(-(x+5)^2 / 4.5) for A in {1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 2.5, 3.0}. Five amplitudes — agent picks at least 3 to sweep within budget.
S3.ground_truth: Karpman-Maslov: number of emitted bright solitons N_sol approximately equals integer part of (A*sigma*sqrt(2)) for sigma=1.5/sqrt(2). Expected: A=1.0 -> 0-1, A=1.5 -> 1-2, A=2.0 -> 2-3, A=2.5 -> 3, A=3.0 -> 3-4.
S3.stress_question: What is the practical soliton-emission threshold for this Gaussian IC under Madelung-Psi split-step? Compare to Karpman-Maslov prediction. At what A does the method itself fail (rather than the physics)?
S3.parameters_to_sweep: Amplitudes A in {1.0, 2.0, 3.0} at minimum. dt fixed at 0.001.

S4.title: NLS defocusing dark soliton
S4.ic: Psi(x,0) = tanh(x). (Dark soliton, density |Psi|^2 = tanh^2(x) has a zero at x=0 — this stress-tests N->0 handling.)
S4.ground_truth: Stationary dark soliton, density profile tanh^2(x) preserved exactly. Phase jumps by pi across x=0.
S4.stress_question: How does Madelung-Psi handle the N=0 point at x=0? Does direct (N, phi) blow up? What regularization is needed? Are periodic-vs-Dirichlet boundary conditions an issue?
S4.parameters_to_sweep: Try Madelung-Psi (with phase jump) and direct (N, phi) (with regularization N->max(N, eps), eps in {1e-6, 1e-4, 1e-2}).

S5.title: B-NLS on Mcs: focusing bright soliton with matched u = N*phi_x
S5.ic: N(x,0) = 2 * sech^2(sqrt(2)*(x+5)), phi(x,0) = 0.25*x (so phi_x = 0.25 everywhere), u(x,0) = 0.25 * N(x,0). [On Mcs: m = u - N*phi_x = 0.]
S5.ground_truth: If the compound-soliton hypothesis is right, the soliton structure propagates approximately rigidly with the boost. Mass conserved exactly. m(x,t) should stay close to zero.
S5.stress_question: Does Strang-splitting B-NLS (Madelung-Psi for NLS sector + RK4 or upwind for u equation) preserve Mcs? Does ||m(t)||_2 grow numerically? What is the natural method coupling between the two sub-systems?
S5.parameters_to_sweep: Time-splitting strategy: Strang first vs Lie-first; coupling order; dt in {0.001, 0.0005}.

S6.title: B-NLS off Mcs: relaxation from u perturbation (key research question)
S6.ic: Same N, phi as S5. u(x,0) = 0.25 * N(x,0) + epsilon * cos(2*pi*x/30) for epsilon in {0.05, 0.2}. [Off Mcs by amount eps.]
S6.ground_truth: User's reported observation: ||m(t)|| should decay. Decay rate unknown — this is the research question. Try at least one epsilon and characterize the decay (exponential, polynomial, oscillatory).
S6.stress_question: Does ||m(t)||_2 decay? What is the decay rate? Does decay rate scale with epsilon? Pick an integration method that is stable for T=12 with conservation properties (mass and energy).
S6.parameters_to_sweep: epsilon in {0.05, 0.2}; integration methods: Strang split with Madelung-Psi (S5 method) vs direct (N, phi, u) IMEX.

S7.title: B-NLS: Burgers bore in u colliding with NLS bright soliton in N
S7.ic: u(x,0) = 1.0 * (1 - tanh(x/0.5))/2 (smoothed bore, u_L=1, u_R=0). N(x,0) = sech^2(x+8) (soliton at x=-8). phi(x,0) = 0.6*x (so phi_x = 0.6 — soliton moves toward bore).
S7.ground_truth: Unknown. The interaction could be transmission, reflection, or capture. Use to characterize which methods handle the shock-soliton coupling.
S7.stress_question: Bore in u creates a steep gradient. Pure spectral on u will Gibbs-oscillate. Try MUSCL-Godunov on u + Madelung-Psi on (N, phi). Does this split work? At what time does the soliton encounter the bore?
S7.parameters_to_sweep: Method combinations: (1) all-spectral all-RK4 [will fail at bore], (2) MUSCL-Godunov u + spectral (N, phi) RK4, (3) MUSCL-Godunov u + Madelung-Psi (N, phi).

S8.title: B-NLS: low-density 'hole' — quantum pressure singularity stress test
S8.ic: N(x,0) = sech^2(x-5) + 0.001 (a sech^2 soliton plus tiny background — so N is small at x=-5 region). phi(x,0) = 0.1*x. u(x,0) = 0.1*N(x,0). Where N is small, (sqrt(N))_xx / sqrt(N) becomes singular numerically.
S8.ground_truth: Unknown. The system may evolve smoothly (if quantum pressure is well-handled) or develop singular behavior.
S8.stress_question: How small can N get before the chosen method fails? Madelung-Psi should handle this well (Psi=sqrt(N+0.001)*e^{i*phi} stays bounded); direct (N, phi) almost certainly fails. Where does each method break?
S8.parameters_to_sweep: Try Madelung-Psi vs direct (N, phi). For direct, try regularizations eps in {1e-6, 1e-3}. Report min_N over the run for each.
